fborg_description_raw: Circle the footbag in between toe kicks.
Kick the footbag with the inside surface of the foot with the kicking leg tucked behind the support leg.
Kick the footbag with the calf of the leg.
Jump and kick the footbag with the inside surface of the foot.
Jump and kick the footbag with the outside surface of the foot.
Delay the footbag on the forehead.

Frigidosis is an inside delay caught behind your body, but not in the cross-body position. The best way to visualize it is to go for an osis, make the catch, but don't spin. So, you "freeze" the bag in a non-cross-body position.
Delay the footbag on the top of the head. Look slightly upwards to the get the top of the head perfectly flat.
Delay the footbag on the inside surface of the foot.
Delay the footbag on the top of the thigh.
Delay the footbag on the back of the neck.
Delay the footbag on the outside surface of the foot.
Delay the footbag on the peak of your cap/hat. This can be dismounted by a flick of the head or 'pinging' your peak.
Delay the footbag on the shoulder. Use your cheek at first to help to catch the footbag.
Kick the footbag with the sole of the foot.
Spin around in between kicks/delays.
Delay the footbag on the top of the shoe (above the toes).
Circle the footbag in between toe kicks.
Kick the footbag with the inside surface of the foot with the kicking leg tucked behind the support leg.
Kick the footbag with the calf of the leg.
Jump and kick the footbag with the inside surface of the foot.
Jump and kick the footbag with the outside surface of the foot.
Delay the footbag on the forehead.

Frigidosis is an inside delay caught behind your body, but not in the cross-body position. The best way to visualize it is to go for an osis, make the catch, but don't spin. So, you "freeze" the bag in a non-cross-body position.
Delay the footbag on the top of the head. Look slightly upwards to the get the top of the head perfectly flat.
Delay the footbag on the inside surface of the foot.
Delay the footbag on the top of the thigh.
Delay the footbag on the back of the neck.
Delay the footbag on the outside surface of the foot.
Delay the footbag on the peak of your cap/hat. This can be dismounted by a flick of the head or 'pinging' your peak.
Delay the footbag on the shoulder. Use your cheek at first to help to catch the footbag.
Kick the footbag with the sole of the foot.
Spin around in between kicks/delays.
Delay the footbag on the top of the shoe (above the toes).

Now jump off the right leg and kick the footbag with the inside surface of the right foot and land on this leg.

[xbd] [uns] Flying Clipper* 2 adds	 	Jump and kick the footbag with the inside surface of the foot with the kicking leg tucked behind the jumping leg. Point toes at their max to create the easiest stalling surface be

fborg_description_raw: From a toe or a clipper delay, set the bag straight up about waist high. As you plant your set leg, bring your support leg up and over the bag from out to in (a reverse miraging motion). Plant the dexterity leg and catch the bag on the toe of the setting foot.

fborg_description_raw: Jump and kick the footbag with the knee tucked behind the kicking leg.

fborg_description_raw: Pass the support leg over the footbag and catch on a delay. E.g., from a left toe stall, hop off the right leg and bring it over the top of the footbag and catch it on a right toe delay.

fborg_description_raw: From a toe delay or a clipper delay, set the bag straight up, and swing the support leg from in to out over the footbag, catching it with the setting leg on a toe delay. The set from a clipper delay is easier once you know how to do a clipper. Note: with a toe-set mirage, the support leg has to first swing under, then back over the bag!
operational_notation: SET > OP IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]

fborg_description_raw: Delay the footbag on the toes on its way down to the ground and continue the downward motion keeping the leg perfectly straight. Keeping a smooth motion swing your leg back and bend at the knee flicking the footbag over your head to the ground in front of you. The key to the pendulum is smooth acceleration while keeping your leg straight as long as physically possible.

fborg_description_raw: A miraging clipper delay. From a clipper- quickly plant the setting leg and bring the support leg over the bag from in to out and in one motion- keeping this leg close to the other leg- into a clipper delay on the other side. Or, setting from left toe, quickly plant the left leg and bring the right leg up and around the bag from in to out. Turn slightly in the direction of the dexterity (in this case, to the right) to help bring the right foot back around into the cross body position to end the move on a right foot clipper delay. Video demonstration available!

You must in this case circle the footbag from the left of the footbag to the front and finally to the right ending on a left toe delay. fborg_description_raw: Spin into a clipper delay. It is easiest to set this from a clipper stall on the other foot or from a toe delay with the other foot, straight in front of you (not behind you). Keep your head down with your eyes on the footbag until just before it lands on the inside surface of your right foot. Then turn quickly to the left (counterclockwise) and catch the footbag on a clipper delay. Video demonstration available!

fborg_description_raw: Jump and kick the footbag with the inside surface of the foot with the kicking leg tucked behind the jumping leg. It's easiest to land on the same leg that kicks the bag. This move is also called "Jester".

fborg_job_raw: SET > OP OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]
fborg_description_raw: Pass the support leg over the footbag and catch on a delay. E.g., from a left toe stall, hop off the right leg and bring it over the top of the footbag and catch it on a right toe delay.
operational_notation: SET > OP OUT [DEX] > SAME TOE [DEL]

fborg_formula_raw: SET > OP IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
fborg_job_raw: SET > OP IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
fborg_description_raw: From a toe delay or a clipper delay, set the bag straight up, and swing the support leg from in to out over the footbag, catching it with the setting leg on a toe delay. The set from a clipper delay is easier once you know how to do a clipper. Note: with a toe-set mirage, the support leg has to first swing under, then back over the bag!
operational_notation: SET > OP IN [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]

fborg_formula_raw: SET > SAME or OP OUT [DEX] > OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
fborg_job_raw: SET > SAME or OP OUT [DEX] > OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
fborg_description_raw: A leg over straight to a clipper delay. E.g., from a right toe set the footbag up and slightly towards yourself. Hop off your right leg and pass your left leg over the footbag- delay the footbag with the inside surface of the right foot in a cross body position ie a clipper delay.
notation_primary: SET > SAME or OP OUT [DEX] > OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
operational_notation: SET > SAME or OP OUT [DEX] > OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]

fborg_formula_raw: SET > (back or front) SPIN [BOD] > SAME or OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
fborg_job_raw: SET > (back or front) SPIN [BOD] > SAME or OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
fborg_description_raw: Spin into a clipper delay. It is easiest to set this from a clipper stall on the other foot or from a toe delay with the other foot, straight in front of you (not behind you). E.g., from a right toe set, set straight in front of you, about waist high, and turn to the left so the footbag passes behind your back... Keep your head down with your eyes on the footbag until just before it lands on the inside surface of your right foot. Then turn quickly to the left (counterclockwise) and catch the footbag on a clipper delay. Video demonstration available!
notation_primary: SET > (back or front) SPIN [BOD] > SAME or OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]
operational_notation: SET > (back or front) SPIN [BOD] > SAME or OP CLIP [XBD] [DEL]

fborg_formula_raw: CLIP > SAME IN [PDX] [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
fborg_job_raw: CLIP > SAME IN [PDX] [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
fborg_description_raw: E.g., from a right clipper delay, set the bag so it's moving across your body from left to right, then bring your right leg around the left leg and around the footbag as it falls. You must in this case circle the footbag from the left of the footbag to the front and finally to the right ending on a left toe delay. Basically the paradox leg completes a tight sharp 'S' shape with the support leg in the first loop and the footbag in the second loop of the letter 'S'.
operational_notation: CLIP > SAME IN [PDX] [DEX] > OP TOE [DEL]
